title: linaria munbyana var. pygmaea
intro: Linaria munbyana var. pygmaea, also known as Dwarf Toadflax, is a small, annual plant species belonging to the figwort family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Linaria munbyana var. pygmaea include its ability to soothe digestive issues and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into infusions, tinctures, or dried flowers for use in teas and remedies, though its usage should be approached with caution due to potential side effects.
